conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can assist you accurately.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for reaching out! I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

To get started, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - their name is Alex and they're 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I understand you have one child named Alex who is 18 years old.

Since Alex is over 16, their eligibility depends on whether they're in approved education or training. Could you please tell me: **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education. They're doing their A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! A-levels at sixth form college would generally count as approved education.

Now I need to understand your relationship with Alex. Could you please tell me: **Do you live with Alex, or if not, do you contribute financially to their upkeep (such as for food, clothes, or pocket money)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you! And one final question to complete the assessment: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:
- Alex is 18 years old and studying A-levels at sixth form college, which counts as approved education
- Child Benefit continues for children under 20 who stay in approved education or training
- You live with Alex in the UK, which means you're responsible for them
- You meet all the necessary eligibility criteria

You can proceed with your claim for Child Benefit. 

**Please note:** If Alex stops their education or training, starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week, or starts claiming certain benefits in their own right, your Child Benefit may be affected. Also, if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is in approved education (doing A-levels at sixth form college). According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK and is therefore responsible for them. All eligibility criteria are met: (1) Alex is under 20 years old, (2) Alex is in approved education, (3) the claimant is responsible for Alex (lives with them), and (4) the claimant lives in the UK.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (18 years old), who lives with them in Manchester, UK. Alex is currently studying A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility continues for children under 20 who remain in approved education or training. The claimant meets all the necessary criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Alex is currently studying A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility continues for children under 20 who remain in approved education or training. The claimant meets all the necessary criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
